message:
feat(undo): Cx-first execute_pane_close — end-to-end cx chain (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 63. Threads cx into the pane_close strategy
handler, closing the last non-Cx gap in the dispatch path for
that strategy.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `UndoExecutor::execute_pane_close_with_cx(cx, request, action,
    undo)` — Cx-first parallel of `execute_pane_close`. Two
    checkpoint seams PLUS routes wezterm calls through the
    Cx-first trait methods:
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` before `get_pane_with_cx` (tick 44
        trait extension).
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` before `kill_pane_with_cx` (tick 45
        trait extension). CRITICAL SEAM: this is the last
        cancellation point before the pane is destructively
        killed.
      - `mark_undone` remains on the ambient path — `StorageHandle`
        has no `_with_cx` surface (147 async fns without cx) and
        a cancel there after a successful kill would leave an
        inconsistent state (pane gone but audit flag unset).
        Deferring the audit-write cx gate until storage gains
        `_with_cx` is the right tradeoff.

  * `execute_with_cx` pane_close arm now calls
    `execute_pane_close_with_cx(cx, ...)` instead of the ambient
    `execute_pane_close`. workflow_abort arm still calls the
    ambient `execute_workflow_abort` — that's a larger follow-on
    (WorkflowRunner doesn't yet have `_with_cx` surface).

End-to-end Cx-first chain wired up by this tick:

    execute_with_cx (tick 63)
    → execute_pane_close_with_cx (tick 64, this commit)
    → wezterm.get_pane_with_cx (tick 44 trait extension)
    → wezterm.kill_pane_with_cx (tick 45 trait extension)
    → mark_undone (ambient storage — pending storage cx surface)

Regression test (new) `execute_with_cx_pane_close_end_to_end`:

  * Mirrors the legacy `pane_close_undo_closes_existing_pane`
    topology: seed pane_id=66, seed action with pane_close undo
    metadata, add MockWezterm pane, drive executor via
    `execute_with_cx`.
  * Asserts:
    - Outcome == Success, target_pane_id == Some(66).
    - MockWezterm.get_pane(66) returns PaneNotFound (pane was
      killed via kill_pane_with_cx).
    - `get_action_undo(action_id)` returns record with
      `undone_at.is_some()` AND
      `undone_by == Some("cx-operator")` (the actor passed via
      UndoRequest).
  * If any link regressed — if the trait-default _with_cx
    delegation accidentally bypassed kill_pane_with_cx, or if the
    pre-kill checkpoint fired incorrectly — the assertions would
    fire. On MockWezterm the trait-default happens to match the
    legacy behavior, so this test is a structural guard; a
    future concrete WeztermHandle with different cx semantics
    would diverge.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * undo:: — 96 passed / 0 failed (tick 63: 95 -> tick 64: 96,
    +1 new end-to-end Cx test; all 2 tick-63 parity tests still
    green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (1m37s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
